entry.title: Ariba Central Invoice Management – Automatic Invoice Parking in Public Cloud (Scope Item 4N6)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

we are currently working with Ariba Central Invoice Management and would like to configure it in a way that incoming invoices are automatically parked (preposted) so that an SAP workflow can be triggered based on the parked invoice document in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

At the moment, whenever the background check is successful, the invoices are automatically posted, which bypasses the workflow process.

We are working within Scope Item 4N6, and the goal is to ensure the workflow runs directly in the Public Cloud environment.

Here is the related SAP Help documentation:
Central Invoice Management – Invoice Processing

Has anyone managed to configure this behavior successfully? Any advice or insights would be highly appreciated!

Best regards,
Aljoscha

Hi Colleague,

in method WDDOMODIFYVIEW, there is an importing reference VIEW (type ref to IF_WD_VIEW).

From here you can access the method IF_WD_CONTROLLER~GET_MESSAGE_MANAGER, which returns a reference of type IF_WD_MESSAGE_MANAGER.

Simon

entry.title: Re: Error when creating a PIR from the RFP Award in Produ...
entry.content:
Hello, thank you for reaching out.
The PIR can only be sent when the vendor has been synchronized from S/4 to SLP and from there to Sourcing, the vendor is active, and has a SAP Vendor ID. Even if the supplier can be invited to an event, a PIR cannot be generated until the synch is done. Hence please make sure supplier has been synchronized and try to send the PIR again.

entry.title: Re: Dual control sensitive fields for vendor master in sap
entry.content:
Dear Mohammed Nafees,

  • Confirmation of changes on sensitive fields in customer/vendor master data is required during automatic payment run and cheque printing. There is no need to confirm the customer/vendor changes during invoice posting/manual payment.
  • The system only checks unconfirmed changes if online payments with forms (for example outgoing checks) are created during manual process. This is not the case in "normal" manual posting and clearing transactions, so that unconfirmed changes are not checked.

Kindly refer this KBA : Dual Control Sensitive Fields for Vendor Master in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Purpose Codes in Payment Media in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Merle,

there is no need to enter the Payment Reason into both Business Partner AND invoice. One source is enough.

It is difficult to find out the issue without looking at your settings, but make sure that there are no invalid conditions anywhere above the element where you are mapping the Purpose Code. You can use the Trace Tool to verify that:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/analyze-the-processing-of-outgoing-payment-files-from-map-format-data/ba-p/13548449

Also make sure that:

1. You are using the same Application Area on your Payment Format and in the Purpose Codes mapping table

2. You are mapping the correct field (FPAYHX-SND_CTRY_CODE / FPAYHX-REC_CTRY_CODE)

Kind regards,

Ondrej

entry.title: Re: Session 5 Recap & Highlights - Enterprise Architects guide on SAP BW Modernization
entry.content:
@appel_solar_dk  - Thank you for your candid feedback—it’s truly appreciated. You’re absolutely right that simply moving BW to the cloud doesn’t automatically equate to modernization, especially when the underlying core remains rooted in decades-old architecture.  That said, the intent with this session as twofold: first, to help organizations get clarity on transition paths (whether to BW/4HANA or DataSphere), and second, to introduce architectural thinking around decoupling, governance, and data productization. 

Hopefully, upcoming sessions in the series will push the envelope a bit further, particularly around innovation layers (like semantic integration or AI-driven orchestration), rather than just platform repositioning. Your feedback definitely helps steer the dialogue in that direction.

cc: @JaSoN_Luo

entry.title: Requirement to Implement Material-Based PR Release Strategy (2-Level / 3-Level)
entry.content:
1️⃣ Define characteristics

  • Create a characteristic (e.g. ZMAT_CHECK) of type CHAR / NUM as needed.
  • The value of this characteristic will represent whether the material code exists in the table or not. Example:
    • Y → Exists → 3-level
    • N → Not exists → 2-level

2️⃣ Create a custom table

  • A Z-table (e.g. ZPR_MAT_CHECK) where you maintain material codes that require 3-level release.

MATNR

10000001

10000002

10000003

3️⃣ Enhance the release strategy determination

  • In user-exit / BAdI (like MEREQ001 or MEREQ_BADI_RELEASE_STRATEGY for PR):
    • Write logic to check if PR material code exists in ZPR_MAT_CHECK.
    • If exists → set characteristic ZMAT_CHECK = Y.
    • If not exists → set ZMAT_CHECK = N.

4️⃣ Release strategy setup

  • Maintain two release strategies:
    • Strategy 1 (2-level) → When ZMAT_CHECK = N
    • Strategy 2 (3-level) → When ZMAT_CHECK = Y

  e_characteristics-zmat_check = 'Y'. " 3-level

  e_characteristics-zmat_check = 'N'. " 2-level

 

 

 

Z-table

 

Field Name

Data Element

Data Type

Length

Key

Description

MATNR

MATNR

CHAR

18

✔

Material Number

 

Important Notes:

 

  1. The table check is performed:
    • When PR is created
    • When PR is changed
    • When release strategy is simulated

entry.title: Re: Multiple Prices for a product
entry.content:
Hello @candidochris 

Thank you for your question

Managing multiple price types like Purchase Cost, Retail Price, or Jobber Price isn’t handled through classic price lists in S4/HANA public cloud, like in Business One.

Instead, common options are,

  • Purchase Info Records for supplier-specific purchase prices
  • Condition Records using pricing procedures for sales-side prices (Retail, Jobber, etc.)
  • For non-transactional prices (e.g., Minimum-Advertised Price), these can be stored as custom fields or in a custom business object if needed

Standard purchase and sales prices are managed via Info Records and Condition Records
Additional prices like Retail or MAP can be handled with custom fields or extensions

There’s no standard mass price list functionality like B1, but pricing can be managed via conditions, master data extensions, or APIs depending on your process.

You can check below SAP Help documentation for your references
SAP Help – Pricing in S/4HANA Public Cloud

Recommended SAP Learning Resources:

 SAP Learning Journey – S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Sourcing & Procurement
Includes how to manage Purchase Info Records, supplier pricing, and master data relevant for purchase-side price handling

 SAP Learning Journey – S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Sales Pricing
Covers condition records, pricing procedures, and setup for customer pricing

SAP Learning Journey – Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
Shows how to handle custom fields, custom business objects, or additional price attributes not covered by standard pricing

Hope it helps

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: SAP 分析云经验分享：如何批量修改日历流程被分派者
entry.content:
SAP 分析云经验分享：如何批量修改日历流程被分派者 

一、介绍 

在当今数字化驱动、企业追求卓越运营的时代，SAP 分析云作为一款功能强大的综合性工具，正全方位助力企业提升决策效能与管理秩序。它承载着数据可视化、预测分析、规划以及协作等多重使命，为企业勾勒出一幅智能管理的宏伟蓝图。 

然而，随着企业版图的拓展、业务流程的精细化以及团队协作的深度化，我们对 SAP 分析云各项功能的期许也在不断攀升。聚焦于 SAP 分析云中的日历功能，尽管它在事件安排、流程追踪等方面表现出色，但目前的筛选机制存在一定的局限性，筛选涉及事件相关人员时，仅能依据 “我的角色” 进行筛选，难以满足企业多元且复杂的管理诉求。 

试想这样的情境：当员工 A 因休假暂别岗位，事件 1 的流程负责人 B 想要迅速梳理出该事件下，由 A 负责且尚未完成的子任务，并重新调配人力，却因为筛选功能的限制，操作时会遇到一些困难；当员工 A 离职，日历管理员肩负起在海量日历数据中，逐一查找 A 身影并进行批量替换的重任，面对冗长且繁琐的操作流程，效率有所降低。为了解决这一痛点，我开发了一个使用了日历API脚本的故事应用。这篇文章中，我将为大家详细介绍。 

二、如何使用“批量修改日历流程被分派者”应用 

使用该故事应用前可在该故事页面查看相关故事应用的使用说明。 

1、要执行此操作，您需要具备以下权限： 

  • 分析模型的“读”权限 
  • 公共文件的“管理”权限 
  • 私有文件的“读”权限 

2、请按照以下步骤获取父日历流程的事件 ID： 

  • 点击导航栏中的“日历”，并选择您要编辑的事件。 

  • 当事件页面打开时，复制 浏览器地址栏中URL中“eventId=” 后面的部分以获取事件 ID。 

或者： 

  • 选中事件后，点击右上角的“详细信息”，在弹出菜单栏的右上角，点击“更多”，然后点击“事件设置”。 

  • 如果未勾选“允许外部 API 访问”选项，请勾选该选项。 

 

  • 然后点击“显示详情”以查看并复制事件 ID。 

3、将事件 ID 粘贴到文本框中。 

4、使用下拉菜单选择您要替换的被分派者。 

5、使用下拉菜单选择您要添加的新被分派者。 

6、点击 “Confirm and Replace” 按钮。 

  • 对于替换成功的事件，系统会弹出绿色窗口以提示替换完成。 
  • 任务全部完成后，系统会弹出灰色窗口，提示“Finished processing all processes and tasks”表示任务已全部完成。 

  • 如果在替换整个流程的过程中存在未被替换成功的事件，则会提示未替换成功的事件数量以及对应的事件ID和事件名称。 

三、注意事项 

1、仅可在父流程中使用，不支持其他类型的日历事件。如果在输入框中输入非流程事件的事件ID，如常规任务的事件ID，则会提示“Only events with the event type of "Process" can be modified. Please enter the Event ID of an event with the event type of "Process".” 

2、无法替换状态为“已取消、失败、成功或部分成功”的任务的指派对象。 

3、无法替换数据锁定任务、数据操作任务、多操作任务、输入任务或发布任务的指派对象。 

四、代码实现 

接下来将对主要的实现逻辑进行介绍 。Calendar提供了比较丰富的集成API ，通过利用相关 API 可实现以上功能。主要逻辑步骤如下： 

1、变量初始化与界面设置 

在实现该功能之前，需要在故事应用中配置“日历集成”，并完成变量的初始化。以下是部分关键变量的说明： 

RemovedAssignee // 想要替换的被分派者 
AddedAssignee // 想要添加的被分派者 
calendarEventId // 相关日历的事件 ID 
removeAssigneeFilterSelection // 下拉菜单实现移除assignee的脚本变量 
addAssigneeFilterSelection // 下拉菜单实现添加assignee的脚本变量   
count // 用来记录没有替换成功的事件的数量 
arrayFailedEvent  // 替换失败的事件集合（事件Id） 
arrayFailedEventName //替换失败的时间集合（事件名称）

同时，可以对加载状态的呈现方式和系统消息提示类型进行相应的预设与配置。 

2、获取事件对象并进行校验 

首先，根据 calendarEventId 获取对应的事件对象 task，并对其存在性进行校验。随后判断该事件的类型是否为“流程类型”（Process）：若类型不符，则提示用户仅支持对“流程类型”的事件进行操作，并中止后续执行。 

var task = calIntegration1.getCalendarEventById(calendarEventId); 
if(task === undefined){ 
Application.showMessage( 
ApplicationMessageType.Warning, 
"The event ID you entered is incorrect. Please verify and re-enter it." 
); 
//用来控制加载状态   
   Application.hideBusyIndicator(); 
return; 
} 
//添加一个判断 判断用户输入事件的事件类型是流程还是其他     
if(task.getType() !== CalendarTaskType.Process ){ 
Application.showMessage( 
ApplicationMessageType.Warning, 
"Only events with the event type of \"Process\" can be modified. Please enter the Event ID of an event with the event type of \"Process.\"" 
); 
//用来控制加载状态    
Application.hideBusyIndicator(); 
return; 
}

3、处理根流程任务 

将事件对象转换为 CalendarProcess 类型。 

对根流程执行被分派者移除（removeAssignee）操作。 

  • 如果移除成功，则执行添加新负责人（addAssignee）。 
  • 根据操作成功与否，输出对应的提示。 
  • 如果失败，记录失败信息并加入失败数组。 
var task_process = cast(Type.CalendarProcess,task); 
// 创建队列并初始化（使用数组模拟队列） 
var eventQueue = ArrayUtils.create(Type.CalendarProcess);  
// 将根流程加入队列 
eventQueue.push(task_process);  
var assigneeRemoved_root = task_process.removeAssignee(RemovedAssignee);  
//用来控制加载状态  
Application.hideBusyIndicator();  
//判断根流程是否删除assignee成功 
 if(assigneeRemoved_root) {  
    Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success, "Assignee has been successfully removeded to Calendar Event " ); 
     //只有根流程assignee删除成功后才会给根流程添加assignee        
      var assigneeAdded_root = task_process.addAssignee(AddedAssignee);     
    //判断根流程是否添加assignee成功     
    if(assigneeAdded_root) {   
           Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success, "Assignee has been successfully addded to Calendar Event " ); 
     }   
     else {   
           Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "Assignee has failed to be added to Calendar Event "); 
     }  
}   
   else {   
       Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "Assignee has failed to be removed to Calendar Event "); 
       count++; 
       arrayFailedEventName.push(calIntegration1.getCalendarEventById(calendarEventId).getName());  
       arrayFailedEvent.push(calendarEventId); 
   }

4、广度优先遍历所有的子流程和子任务 

对队列 eventQueue 进行广度优先遍历所有子事件。 

对每一个子事件执行： 

  • 获取其对象。 
  • 如果是流程类型，继续加入队列遍历其子项。 
  • 对每个子项尝试移除原被分派者，成功后尝试添加新被分派者。 
  • 成功与否都记录日志和信息，失败的加入失败数组。 
// 广度优先遍历处理所有流程    
while (eventQueue.length > 0) { 
    // 从队列头部取出当前流程     
    var currentProcess = eventQueue.shift(); 
    // 获取当前流程的所有子项（子流程或子任务）  
    var childrenIds = currentProcess.getChildren(); 
    // 遍历所有子项    
    for (var i = 0; i < childrenIds.length; i++) { 
        var childEvent = calIntegration1.getCalendarEventById(childrenIds[i]);  
        // 如果是子流程，加入队列继续处理其子项    
        if (childEvent.getType() === CalendarTaskType.Process) { 
            var childProcess = cast(Type.CalendarProcess, childEvent); 
            eventQueue.push(childProcess); // 将子流程加入队列   
        }     
        // 对当前子项（无论流程还是任务）执行移除操作   
        var assigneeRemoved = childEvent.removeAssignee(RemovedAssignee); 
        // 处理删除操作结果   
        if (assigneeRemoved) { 
            Application.showMessage( 
                ApplicationMessageType.Success, 
                "Assignee removed from: " + childEvent.getId() 
            ); 
           var assigneeAdded = childEvent.addAssignee(AddedAssignee); 
           // 处理添加操作结果     
     if (assigneeAdded) { 
         Application.showMessage( 
         ApplicationMessageType.Success, 
         "Assignee added to: " + childEvent.getId() 
          );  
     } 
     else { 
         Application.showMessage( 
         ApplicationMessageType.Error, 
         "Failed to add to: " + childEvent.getId() 
         ); 
     }  
  }  
       else { 
               count++; 
               arrayFailedEvent.push(childEvent.getId());                                    arrayFailedEventName.push(calIntegration1.getCalendarEventById(childEvent.getId()).getName());  
       Application.showMessage( 
                ApplicationMessageType.Error, 
                "Failed to remove from: " + childEvent.getId() 
                                 ); 
       }    
    } 
}

5、输出错误信息和结果提示 

  •  如果有失败的事件，显示失败数量。 
  • 对每一个失败事件，显示事件名和 ID。 
  • 最后输出“任务处理完成”提示信息。 
var count_string = ConvertUtils.numberToString(count); 
// 判断是否有未替换成功的事件，如果有，输出未替换成功的事件数量   
if(count !== 0){ 
Application.showMessage( 
               ApplicationMessageType.Warning, 
               "You have \"" + count_string + "\" events where the assignee failed to be replaced successfully."  
              ); 
}  
for (var j = 0; j < count; j++) { 
               warningMessage = warningMessage + "The assignee of event  \"" + arrayFailedEventName[j]  + "\" failed to be replaced successfully.\n"; 
               warningMessage = warningMessage + " the event ID: " + arrayFailedEvent[j] + "\n\n"; 
} 
if (warningMessage) { 
               Application.showMessage( 
               ApplicationMessageType.Warning, 
               warningMessage 
              ); 
} 
//任务执行结束   
Application.showMessage( 
              ApplicationMessageType.Info, 
             "Finished processing all processes and tasks" 
);

五、总结 

本文主要分享了我开发“批量修改日历流程被分派者”故事应用的经验。如果您也遇到了类似的问题，希望可以给您启发。

entry.title: New Danish Tax requirements in 2025 and beyond - How SAP can help
entry.content:
Marc Hoessels (PwC Belgium - Managing Director Tax & ERP Transformations) and Anders Strandet Jepsen (PwC Denmark - Partner)

 

  1. Introduction to the Danish Bookkeeping Act

The Danish Bookkeeping Act, in Danish “Bogføringsloven”, outlines the principles and requirements for digital accounting and bookkeeping in Denmark. It includes several immediate tax-related obligations for businesses, such as:

  • Supporting the automatic sending and receiving of e-invoices in OIOUBL/PEPPOL BIS 
  • Enabling the sharing of bookkeeping data in SAF-T format.

Beyond these tax-related requirements, the Act imposes broader bookkeeping obligations, including but not limited to:

  • Performing necessary tax reconciliations for each tax-relevant filing.
  • Registering all company transactions in compliance with bookkeeping standards.
  • Maintaining an audit trail for recorded transactions.
  • Digitally storing purchase and sales invoices.
  • Conducting a full backup of stored data weekly with an independent party inside the EU that meets IT security standards.

These regulations aim to enhance financial transparency, data security, and compliance within Denmark’s digital bookkeeping framework.

Further, the Act distinguishes between approved and non-approved digital bookkeeping systems, where the requirements can differ and the responsibility to comply can lie with either the company or the ERP provider. For non-approved systems, the responsibility lies with the company.

It is therefore important to determine which category one's bookkeeping system belongs to, and which associated requirements must be met and how to meet them. As this could have an impact on when the requirements are/become applicable (depending on the financial year of the entity):

  • 1 July 2024 (past): Digital bookkeeping applies for approved ERP systems
  • 1 January 2025 (present): Digital bookkeeping applies for non-approved ERP systems for most companies in Denmark
  • 1 January 2026 (future): Digital bookkeeping applies to all entities with net annual turnover over DKK 300,000 (approx. EUR 40,000), including foreign companies with branches in Denmark

SAP has clarified that SAP S/4HANA is not officially recognized as a standard bookkeeping system under the Danish Bookkeeping Act. Therefore, it is the client's responsibility to ensure that they are in compliance with the Danish Bookkeeping Act. To help you with this PwC can perform a ‘compliance validation’. This process helps you identify and address any compliance gaps related to the Danish Bookkeeping Act, which encompasses more than just VAT requirements (such as SAF-T, e-invoicing, etc.). As it also includes requirements related to:

  • Ensuring a control trail for accounting data
  • Availability of banking data
  • Reconciliation of banking data
  • Regular back ups of data
  • …

2. E-Invoice compliance under the Act

As mentioned, companies must be able to automatically send and receive e-invoices in Peppol and OIOUBL format. OIOUBL is a Danish e-invoicing standard maintained by the Danish Business Authority and is typically used for transactions with Danish government entities via the NemHandel platform.

Peppol, another accepted e-invoicing format under the Danish Bookkeeping Act, is a globally recognized standard. To use Peppol, companies need a certified Peppol access point before engaging in Peppol e-invoicing. Additionally, it's crucial to ensure that your customers are registered on the Peppol network; otherwise, your e-invoices may not reach their intended destination.

The requirements in the Danish Bookkeeping Act outlines the requirements to system capabilities. The Danish Tax Agency has not (yet) mandated the use for VAT taxable B2B or B2C transactions, but the legislations are expected to align in the near future (likely to be introduced in 2028). 

Next to the strict Danish obligation, also ViDA is around the corner (2030), which impacts your EU cross border transactions with the EU wide implementation of Digital Reporting Requirements (DRR). One aspect of the DRR is to move cross border trade closer to real-time reporting by abolishing the periodic recapitulative statements (e.g. EU sales listings) and instead mandating the suppliers to report the cross border trade within 10 days of the chargeable event. This coincides with the e-invoicing requirements, that are also part of the DRR, where e-invoices is the new default for invoicing and e-invoices must be issued within the same time frame. 

Since more and more countries have implemented, will implement or plan to implement a kind of e-invoicing, it is a good business decision to look at all obligations in the EU and beyond and try to harmonise your e-invoicing solution(s) which will lower your IT cost & maintenance over time. 

3. SAF-T (Standard Audit File for Tax)

The new Bookkeeping Act requires bookkeeping systems to support the SAF-T international standard file format. This facilitates easier and more secure data exchanges between companies, such as with the Danish Tax Agency, and simplifies switching bookkeeping systems.


Currently, companies are not mandated to regularly submit SAF-T files to authorities. However, bookkeeping systems must be capable of generating, loading, and exporting SAF-T files if requested by the Danish Tax Agency, such as during a VAT or tax inspection. The current obligation requires businesses to produce a SAF-T file on header level.

However, this requirement is likely to be extended to all transactions (purchases and sales) by 2028 and with further reporting requirements in the subsequent years.

4. Implementation in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (hereafter SAP DRC) for DK e-invoicing and SAF-T

SAP offers a solution called SAP Document and Reporting Compliance to assist with indirect tax and e-invoicing reporting obligations globally. 

In Denmark, SAP DRC can generate from a reporting perspective:

  • VAT return (where Electronic submission of the VAT return is on the roadmap for delivery)
  • EC Sales Listings
  • The header details of the Danish SAF-T (SAP intends to deliver full SAF-T, once more root elements become mandatory).

From an e-reporting/e-invoicing perspective:

  • Both Peppol and OIOUBL formats via the Peppol Network
  • Peppol e-order
  • Integration to Nemhandel (on the roadmap for delivery).

entry.title: Navigating the Labyrinth: Choosing the Right Persistent Data Storage for Your Custom Development
entry.content:
Hello All,

During a recent implementation project, I encountered a familiar yet significant dilemma: determining the right approach for persistent data storage in a custom RICEF development. The functional team specifically requested something as intuitive and user-friendly as SM30 for data maintenance—a scenario many of us in the SAP ecosystem can relate to.

While the need for a custom configuration table was clear, the abundance of options in S/4HANA—across both on-premise and cloud environments—made the decision more complex than expected. Add to that the challenge of balancing legacy ECC practices with modern S/4HANA architecture, and the decision-making process becomes even more nuanced.

In this blog, I’ll walk through our real-world scenario, the solutions we considered, and the reasoning behind the final choice. The goal is to offer a concise yet practical guide to help others facing similar decisions in their S/4HANA journey.

Understanding the Landscape: On-Premise vs. Cloud

Before we dive into the specifics, it's crucial to understand that the choice of approach is heavily influenced by your SAP S/4HANA deployment model: on-premise or cloud. The"clean core" strategy, which emphasizes upgrade stability and limiting direct modifications to the core system, significantly shapes the available options, especially in the public cloud. What might be a viable solution on-premise could be entirely unsupported or not recommended in the cloud.

Let's break down the options by deployment model.

S/4HANA On-Premise: A Blend of Tradition and Modernity
In an S/4HANA on-premise environment, you have the flexibility to leverage both traditional ABAP development paradigms and newer, cloud-aligned approaches. This often leads to the dilemma that we faced: which path to take?

1. The Classical ABAP Approach: The Tried and True (with a Caveat)


This is perhaps the most familiar territory for many seasoned ABAP developers. It involves creating custom tables directly in the ABAP Dictionary (transaction SE11) and then generating a maintenance dialog using the Table Maintenance Generator (TMG) viaSE11 or SE54. End-users can then maintain data using the ubiquitous transaction SM30. You can even create custom transaction codes (SE93) for direct access, enhancing user experience and security. TMG also offers "events" where you can embed custom ABAP logic for validations, defaulting values, and other business requirements.

Features:

  • Full Control: You get granular control over table design, field properties, and database attributes. This is ideal for highly complex data models or scenarios requiring specific performance optimizations at the database level.
  • Familiarity: SM30 is a standard and widely known transaction, making it familiar to many SAP users for data maintenance.
  • Custom Logic: TMG events provide hooks for embedding custom ABAP logic, allowing for robust validations and business rules.

When to Use:

  • When maximum control and flexibility over table design and implementation are paramount.
  • For highly complex data models or specific performance optimizations that can only be achieved at the ABAP Dictionary level.
  • When existing SAP GUI-based processes are acceptable or preferred by the end-users, or when the development effort for a Fiori UI is not justified for the specific use case.

2. The Custom TVARVC Table Approach: Simple Configurations, Simple Maintenance

This approach involves creating a custom table (typically starting with 'Z' or 'Y') that mimics the structure of the standard TVARVC table. This custom table is then used to store application-specific parameters or simple configuration values. Maintenance is usually performed via Custom SAP GUI report/ transactions similar to STVARV or SM30.

Features:

  • Simple Configuration: Ideal for storing key-value pairs or simple ranges, such as company codes, plant-specific parameters, threshold values, or lists for custom logic. It's excellent for preventing hardcoding of values in ABAP programs, userexits, and reports.
  • Reusable Framework: A single custom TVARVC-style table and its supporting maintenance report can be designed as a reusable framework, enabling consistent configuration handling across multiple custom developments.

When to Use:

  • For simple configuration parameters that control custom ABAP developments.
  • When a quick and straightforward solution with minimal initial development for table structure and maintenance UI is needed.
  • When end-users are comfortable with SAP GUI for configuration maintenance.

3. Custom Business Objects (CBOs): The Modern On-Premise Approach

Custom Business Objects (CBOs) represent a modern and increasingly recommended method for defining custom data structures and behaviors in S/4HANA on-premise. They are managed via the "Custom Business Objects" Fiori app. A significant advantage of CBOs is the automatic generation of a Fiori-based UI for data maintenance, eliminating the need for manual UI development for basic CRUD operations. Each CBO is backed by a system-provisioned database table and inherently exposes an OData service for seamless integration with Fiori applications and other systems. This is essentially a low-code approach to custom tables with integrated Fiori maintenance.

Features:

  • Automatic Fiori UI: The system automatically generates a Fiori-based UI for data maintenance, providing a modern user experience with minimal development effort.
  • OData Service Exposure: CBOs automatically expose an OData service, simplifying integration with other Fiori apps, SAP systems, and external applications.
  • Key User Extensibility: CBOs empower key users to define and manage their owndata structures through a Fiori interface, reducing reliance on traditional ABAP developers for simple requirements.
  • Associations and Logic: Supports associations with other business objects for intricate data models and allows for custom logic (validations, determinations) via cloud BAdIs.

When to Use:

  • When the primary requirement is a user-friendly, modern Fiori interface for custom table maintenance.
  • For rapid development and deployment of maintenance UIs with minimal coding.
  • Well-suited for moderately complex data structures.
  • When you want to empower key users with extensibility capabilities.

4. Fiori Elements with ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model(RAP): The Future of On-Premise Development

This is a powerful and increasingly recommended approach for building Fiori applications, including those for custom table maintenance, in S/4HANA on-premise.RAP provides a standardized way to build OData services for transactional and analytical applications. Fiori Elements then automatically generate UIs based on these OData services and CDS views, significantly accelerating development.

Features:

  • Model-Driven UI: Fiori Elements generate UIs automatically from CDS views andOData services, drastically reducing manual UI coding.
  • RAP for Backend Logic: RAP handles the transactional and analytical logic,including CRUD operations for custom tables, ensuring a robust and scalablebackend.
  • Integrated Development: Development is done in ADT (ABAP Development Toolsin Eclipse), providing a modern development experience.

When to Use:

  • When building new Fiori applications for custom table maintenance in S/4HANA on-premise.
  • For scenarios requiring a robust, scalable, and performant solution that aligns with SAP's strategic direction.
  • When you want to leverage the benefits of automated UI generation and a consistent Fiori user experience.

5. Freestyle SAPUI5 Applications: Ultimate UI Customization


For scenarios requiring highly customized user interfaces or complex interactions not easily achievable with Fiori Elements or CBOs, developers can build freestyle SAPUI5applications. These applications consume OData services (often built using classical ABAP or RAP) that expose the custom table data.

Features:

  • Full UI Control: Developers have complete control over the UI layout, controls, and behavior, allowing for unique branding, complex data entry forms, or highly interactive dashboards.
  • Flexibility: Allows for highly specific UI/UX requirements that go beyond standard Fiori Elements templates.
  • Data Binding: Connects to backend data via OData services, which can be built using classical ABAP (SEGW) or RAP.

When to Use:

  • When your UI/UX requirements are extremely unique, complex, or cannot be met by standard Fiori Elements or CBO-generated UIs.
  • For highly interactive applications that require a bespoke user experience.

On-Premise Summary: Choosing the Right Persistent Storage Method

   

ApproachUI TypeWho Builds Coding RequiredClean Core AlignmentBest Use Case
Classical ABAP (SM30)SAP GUI (SM30)DevelopersMedium❌LowLegacy compatibility, complex tables, TMG event handling
Custom TVARVC TableSAP GUI (STVARV/SM30)DevelopersLow❌LowSimple config parameters, fast implementation
Custom Business Objects (CBOs)Fiori (Auto-generated)Key Users / DevsLow to Medium✅HighRapid deployment of Fiori-based maintenance apps
RAP + Fiori ElementsFiori (Auto-generated)DevelopersMedium to High✅HighModern architecture, scalable, maintainable development
Freestyle SAPUI5 AppFiori (Custom UI)DevelopersHigh✅High Highly customized UI/UX, complex workflows or dashboards

 

S/4HANA Cloud: The "Clean Core" Paradigm

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition), custom development follows strict “clean core” principles—minimizing direct system modifications to ensure upgrade stability. Traditional ABAP methods like SM30 or TVARVC-based tables are not applicable due to the absence of SAP GUI and limited backend extensibility.

Instead, SAP provides modern, cloud-compliant extensibility options:

1. Custom Business Objects (CBOs): Key User-Friendly and Fiori-Ready

CBOs are the recommended way to handle custom data needs in the cloud. Created via the "Custom Business Objects" Fiori app, they support:

  • Auto-generated Fiori UI for maintenance (CRUD)

  • Automatic OData exposure for integration

  • No-code/low-code development suited for business users

  • Upgrade-safe by design

Use When: You need a quick, compliant way to store and maintain custom data with minimal developer effort.

2. ABAP Cloud (Embedded Steampunk) + RAP: For Pro Developers

For advanced scenarios, ABAP Cloud (via Embedded Steampunk) allows professional developers to build applications using RAP within ADT. These apps:

  • Use whitelisted APIs and restricted ABAP syntax

  • Expose OData services for consumption in Fiori Elements

  • Are fully aligned with clean core standards

Use When: You need full control over backend logic or have complex business scenarios beyond what key user tools offer.

3. Custom SAPUI5 on BTP: For Bespoke UI Requirements

For highly customized or interactive UIs, build SAPUI5 apps on SAP BTP, consuming OData services from RAP or CBOs.

Use When: Standard Fiori templates don’t meet your UX needs and you want to keep extensions side-by-side (not inside the core).

Summary

     
ApproachUI TypeWho BuildsCoding RequiredClean Core ComplianceUse Case
CBOAuto Fiori UIKey UsersNo/Low✅HighSimple to moderate custom data needs
RAP + Fiori ElementsAuto Fiori UIDevelopersYes✅HighComplex business logic, developer-led
SAPUI5 on BTPCustom UIDevelopersYes✅Very High (Side-by-side)Bespoke UX or standalone apps

 

Making the Right Choice: A Decision Framework

Choosing the right approach for persistent data storage in your custom RICEF development is a critical decision that impacts development effort, user experience,integration capabilities, performance, security, transportability, and long-term maintainability. To simplify this, consider the following decision framework:

1. Deployment Model First: Are you on S/4HANA On-Premise or S/4HANA Cloud(Public Edition)? This is the most crucial differentiator. Cloud environments are far more restrictive due to the "clean core" strategy.

2. User Experience Requirement: Do your users require a traditional SAP GUI experience (less common now), a modern Fiori app experience, or a highly customized, bespoke UI?

3. Data Model Complexity: Is your custom data simple (key-value pairs), moderately complex (multiple fields, simple associations), or highly complex (intricate relationships, specific performance needs)?

4. Development Effort & Skills: What are your team's existing skills (classical ABAP,RAP, SAPUI5, BTP) and what is the available development budget and timeline?

5. "Clean Core" Adherence: How strictly do you need to adhere to the "clean core"principles for future upgrades and cloud readiness?

Here's a simplified table to guide your decision:

 

Feature/ConsiderationClassical ABAP(On-Premise)Custom TVARVC (On-Premise)CBOs (On-Premise &Cloud)RAP/Fiori Elements(On-Premise &Cloud)FreestyleUI5 (On-Premise &Cloud)
DeploymentOn-PremiseOn-PremiseOn-Premise &CloudOn-Premise &CloudOn-Premise &Cloud
UI Type
SAP		SAP GUI(SM30)SAP GUI(STVARV/SM30)Fiori(Auto-generated)Fiori(Auto-generated)Fiori(Custom)
Data ComplexityHighLowMediumHighHigh
Dev EffortMediumLowMediumMediumHigh
"Clean Core"AlignmentLowLowHighHighHigh(especially via BTP)
Key User ExtensibilityNoNoYesNo(Developer)No(Developer)
OData ExposureManual(SEGW)NoAutomaticAutomaticConsumes OData
Best ForLegacy, complex DB needsSimple configRapid Fiori appsRobust Fiori apps,complex logicUnique UI/UX needs

Final Thoughts

The world of SAP S/4HANA is constantly evolving, and so too are the best practices for custom development. The days of a one-size-fits-all approach are long gone. As ABAP developers and consultants, it's our responsibility to guide our functional colleagues and project teams towards the most appropriate and future-proof solutions.

My recent experience reminded me that while the technical options are abundant, the real challenge lies in understanding the business requirement, the system landscape,and the long-term implications of each choice. By carefully considering the factors outlined above, you can navigate this labyrinth with confidence, ensuring your custom RICEF developments are not only functional but also scalable, maintainable, and aligned with SAP's strategic direction.

Happy coding, and may your custom tables always be clean and performant!

entry.title: Build Apps support accessibility features
entry.content:
Hello,

Does  Build Apps support accessibility features for visually impaired users, such as screen reader compatibility and keyboard navigation?

entry.title: Re: SF Report Story for Performance Distribution
entry.content: Hi Richie, I'm not a mathematician, but it looks like you're trying to divide a number by itself (e.g., 1/1=1) in your "Dis" calculation, which definitely will not work in Stories. Try creating a couple of additional measures instead: Number of Males and Number of Females, and then use them in your calculations. However, it seems like there will be two final measures in the end too, which will influence the visual widget.

entry.title: Re: RDP access point connection doesn't work in CAL Appli...

Hi otavio_soares ,

 

According to our log files your appliance has been provisioned successfully. 

Could you check your IP Address again and verify your ports restrictions? If your IP is different than the ones listed in your Appliance Details -> Virtual Machines section, then please restrict your ports to the correct one. If not, our recommendation is to involve your local IT department and/or GCP account administrator.

For more information, see our documentation .

 

Best regards,

Tsvetinka

entry.title: New Custom Heuristic for Campaign Planning in PP/DS based on Order Quantity Thresholds
entry.content:
Summary

This blog post describes how to create a new custom heuristic for automatic creation of production campaigns from neighboring orders that have the same setup group, for specific customer business case where a minimum and a maximum number of units per campaign must be considered.

The target audience for this blog post will be consulting people, project team members, key users, and subject matter experts who are interested on the heuristic algorithms for PP/DS.

 

Introduction

In Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (PP/DS), production campaign planning allows businesses to group related production orders together for more efficient production scheduling and cost allocation. This is particularly useful in process industries where production lines may require setup or cleaning between processing different production batches or before downtimes. By grouping orders into campaigns, you can optimize the sequence of operations, reduce setup costs, and potentially lower storage costs. 

You mainly use production campaigns in companies that manufacture their products on production lines. Campaign planning functions can combine orders for similar or identical products into production campaigns. This consolidation help reduces setup and storage costs significantly, allowing products to be manufactured in a single manufacturing process over a longer period.

You can decide whether you want to use campaign optimization to plan campaigns or campaign heuristics. If you want to use campaign optimization for your planning, the following requirements must be met:

  • You use only one bottleneck resource within a production data structure model or, if there are more bottleneck resources, they are defined as alternative resources only.
  • There are no cross-order relationships or pegging relationships between the activities at the bottleneck resource.

If these requirements are not met, then you can use campaign heuristics to plan campaigns automatically.

 

Campaign Planning

Campaign planning can utilize either campaign optimization or campaign heuristics. You can plan campaigns in PP/DS as follows:

  • Automatically
    • By using campaign optimization, in which the system creates campaigns. The system tries to find an optimum number and sequence of orders in a campaign, as well as the sequence between the campaigns, while taking setup and storage costs into account.
    • By processing the campaign heuristics in sequence.
  • Manually. You can use the detailed scheduling planning board or campaign transaction to determine the number and sequence of orders in a campaign, as well as the sequence between the campaigns.

During scheduling, the system checks the following campaign-specific requirements for those resources you specified as campaign-relevant:

  • The activities of a campaign cannot be interrupted by activities that do not belong to orders of the same campaign.
  • You can position an order at the beginning or end of a campaign. Setup orders, for example, are always at the beginning of a campaign, and cleaning orders are always at the end.

The system schedules the orders at these resources so above requirements are not violated. Furthermore, the minimum resource commitment is also considered, which means that when scheduling campaigns and their orders, the system tries to occupy as few resources as possible. If you use alternative modes, all activities of a campaign must take place at the same resource.

The system only checks the above campaign requirements for the resources if the following indicators are set:

  • The “Campaign-Relevant” indicator in the resource master.
  • The “Consider Campaign Requirements” indicator in the strategy profile.

Figure 1: “Campaign-Relevant” indicator in the resource master.

Figure 2: “Consider Campaign Requirements” indicator in the strategy profile.

 

Main Tools and Features for Campaign Planning

The main tools and features to manage campaign planning are:

  1. Detailed Scheduling Planning Board: transaction /SAPAPO/CDPS0 provides a graphical view of campaigns and orders, allowing you to plan and schedule campaigns visually.
  2. Campaign transaction: transaction /SAPAPO/PCMT provides a table-based view of campaigns, orders, and dates, offering a more structured approach to campaign planning.
  3. Campaign heuristics: a feature that can be used to optimize the planning of campaigns by automatically generating a finite production plan.

By using these tools, the following campaign functions among others are available:

  • Create campaigns.
  • Dissolve campaigns.
  • Remove orders from campaign.
  • Add orders into existing campaign.
  • Splitting campaigns.
  • Merging campaigns.
  • Move orders from one campaign to another.
  • Create cleaning/setup orders.

Figure 3: Visual representation of a campaign in the detailed scheduling planning board.

Figure 4: Table-based view of a campaign in the campaign transaction.

Figure 5: Example of campaign heuristics functions in the detailed scheduling planning board.

Campaigns are visually represented by bars above the operation bars, covering the operations within the campaign. You can use drag and drop to move the campaign bars on the time axis. The planning functions of the detailed scheduling planning board help you to schedule campaigns. By default, the campaign bar label includes the campaign number, description, and status. You can also access the PP/DS campaign number when using the product view (transaction /SAPAPO/RRP3).

The campaign number comes from an internal number range defined on transaction PCMNO. When saving the campaign, an external campaign number is also assigned containing the S/4HANA campaign number, from a number range defined on transaction CPCN:

Figure 6: Example of internal and external campaign number in PP/DS campaign transaction.

Figure 7: Example of external campaign number in S/4HANA campaign transaction.

When manually creating a campaign, you can select its color from a designated palette of campaign colors (six colors available). When automatically planning campaigns, the system automatically assigns the color specified in the campaign profile. The campaign profiles can be managed either through transaction /SAPAPO/PCMP directly or through the following customizing path: IMG -> Advanced Planning -> Production Campaign Planning --> Maintain Campaign Profile.

Figure 8: Palette of available colors for campaigns visualization.

In the campaign profile, you perform settings which the system uses to create campaigns and for campaign optimization. You create a campaign profile for a certain location and setup group. The “Maximum Number of Charges in a Campaign” specifies the maximum number of orders possible per campaign when using the setup group entered. The “Campaign Color” specifies the display color of the bars that represent the production campaign in the resource chart of the detailed scheduling planning board. The default color for campaigns is violet, which is the one used by default if not campaign profile found by setup group.

Figure 9: Example of settings on the campaign profile.

This automatic coloring based on the campaign profiles will only work for automatic campaigns creation. If users are going to create campaigns manually, then they must enter the color manually or default color will be applied. This means that campaign profiles do not apply for manually created campaigns.

Every production campaign in PP/DS has a status that documents the campaign’s current processing status. The campaign status is derived from the status of the orders contained in the PP/DS campaign:

  • Status Created: The campaign either contains no orders or only orders that have neither been released nor started.
  • Status Released: The campaign contains at least one order that has been released and no order that has been started.
  • Status Started: The campaign contains at least one order that has been started.

The campaign status is also displayed graphically at the campaign bar in the detailed scheduling planning board. The status is indicated by various hatch patterns in the campaign bar.

  • Status Created: Colored, no hatch.
  • Status Released: Colored, diagonal stripe.
  • Status Started: Colored, diagonal crosshatch.

  

Figure 10: Example of campaign bar display on the base of the production campaign status.

 

Standard Heuristics for Production Campaigns

A heuristic is a planning function that executes planning for selected objects using a particular algorithm. In this way, the focus of planning can be products, orders, operations, resources, or line networks. Several standard heuristics are available for campaign planning. You can use them in the production planning run or interactively in the detailed scheduling planning board to process production campaigns. The available heuristics for campaign planning are the following:

  1. Create production campaigns (algorithm SAP_PCM_CRT, function module /SAPAPO/HEUR_PCM_CREATE)

Using this heuristic, you create production campaigns from neighboring orders that manufacture the same product or have the same setup group, depending on the heuristic setting.

  1. Dissolve production campaigns (algorithm SAP_PCM_DIS, function module /SAPAPO/HEUR_PCM_DISSOLVE)

Using this heuristic, you delete production campaigns, for example, before you let PP/DS optimization process your plan. The algorithm deletes as many orders as possible from all campaigns with the selected properties, and then deletes the campaigns that do not contain any orders.

  1. Delete setup/clean-out orders (algorithm SAP_PCM_ODEL, function module /SAPAPO/HEUR_PCM_ORDERS_DELETE)

Using this heuristic, you delete production setup/clean-out orders, for example, before you let PP/DS optimization process your plan. The sequence-dependent setup activity at the beginning of the campaign that had duration 0 as the successor of the setup/clean-out order increases by the value defined in the setup matrix for the new sequence.

  1. Adjust setup/clean-out orders (algorithm SAP_PCM_SRVA, function module /SAPAPO/HEUR_PCM_SERVICE_ADAPT)

Using this heuristic, you create setup/clean-out orders for a campaign. The algorithm deletes all existing setup/clean-out orders and then replaces all existing sequence-dependent setup activities at the beginning of a campaign with a setup/clean-out order.

To use campaign heuristics in the detailed scheduling planning board, you must define a heuristic profile that contains the corresponding campaign heuristics and assign it to the overall profile of the detailed scheduling planning board. This is done through the following customizing path: IMG -> Advanced Planning -> Heuristics -> Maintain Heuristic Profiles.

Figure 11: Example of heuristic profile containing several campaign heuristics.

To call up a heuristic in the detailed scheduling planning board, you select the relevant objects and choose menu option “Functions” > “Heuristics”. To use the heuristic in the production planning run, you specify the processing steps you want to carry out and select the relevant objects. All the campaign heuristic algorithms consider the settings made in the campaign profile and in the definition of the heuristic.

When you define a heuristic, you first select the algorithm for the heuristic and second define the settings for the heuristic. This is done either through transaction /SAPAPO/CDPSC11 directly or through the following customizing path: IMG -> Advanced Planning -> Heuristics -> Maintain Heuristics.

Figure 12: Example of default heuristic settings for heuristic “Create production campaigns”.

To change the default settings relevant to the heuristic, you choose Settings for Heuristic in the detailed scheduling planning board.

Figure 13: Example of heuristic settings maintained on detailed scheduling planning board.

The list with the detailed steps of the overall campaign planning flow with campaign heuristics would the following:

 

Step

Description

1

Planning orders are created by MRP Live.

2

You delete existing campaigns, setup and clean-out orders using the heuristics “Dissolve campaign” and “Delete setup/clean-out orders” since PP/DS optimization cannot process them.

3

You start PP/DS optimization for selected resources (bottleneck resources) in the detailed scheduling planning board or the production planning run.

4

PP/DS optimization creates an order sequence with optimized setup times at the selected resources. Storage costs cannot be considered.

5

Using the heuristic “Create production campaigns”, you let the system create campaigns consisting of orders that follow each other and have the same setup group or product in the detailed scheduling planning board or the production planning run.

6

If you want to use setup or clean-out orders, you execute the heuristic “Adjust setup/clean-out orders” or the function “Adjust setup/clean-out orders” in the detailed scheduling planning board. The system replaces sequence-dependent setup activities by setup or clean-out orders.

7

If required, you correct the planning in the detailed scheduling planning board or the campaign transaction and eliminate campaign alerts . You can assign additional orders to campaign.

8

You fix campaigns if you do not want them to be changed by another automatic planning run. You freeze the scheduling result.

9

If the planning situation changes, you start again with step 2.

10

You convert the planning to process orders for SAP S/4HANA.

 

New custom heuristic for creation of production campaigns based on quantity thresholds

The standard algorithm SAP_PCM_CRT for creation of production campaigns (function module /SAPAPO/HEUR_PCM_CREATE) creates new production campaigns and assigns orders to them. The algorithm assumes that you use finite scheduling and the procedure for activity/order assignation and sequencing is the following:

  1. The algorithm chronologically sorts all activities on the selected campaign-relevant resources in the planning period, defined by the selected time profile.
  2. The algorithm creates production campaigns from all neighboring activities with the following requirements:
    • The activities are not already part of a production campaign.
    • The activities are not fixed.
    • The activities are not deallocated.
    • The activities have the same setup group or produce the same product, depending on the settings for the heuristic.

The algorithm also takes the settings defined in the campaign profile into consideration. For instance, if for certain location and setup group a campaign profile exists, only the defined maximum number of orders possible per campaign are allowed. You can also use the campaign requirement indicator in the settings for the heuristic to determine if campaigns can be interrupted by downtimes, for example.

Customer business case was that they did not want to restrict a maximum number of orders possible per campaign, but they needed to define a minimum and a maximum number of units per campaign. This units will be in the production unit of measure of the orders. The required criteria for campaign aggrupation were the same setup group.

SAP provides a lot of standard heuristics for planning in PP/DS, but also have an option to create your own custom heuristics either through transaction /SAPAPO/CDPSC11 directly or else through the following customizing path IMG -> Advanced Planning -> Heuristics -> Maintain Heuristics.

When creating the new custom heuristic, first step is to select the algorithm for the heuristic. The way to proceed is to copy the standard function module /SAPAPO/HEUR_PCM_CREATE into a custom function module containing the own logic and copy other auxiliar function modules in case custom changes are also required.

Second step is to define the settings for the heuristic. In our business case, the two parameters containing the “Minimum Campaign Units” and the “Maximum Campaign Units” were added. Furthermore, the corresponding “Unit of measure” for these two parameters was also added, to avoid issues with production units different than base units of measure, and to be valid for other scenarios.

Figure 14: Example of default heuristic settings for custom heuristic algorithm.

Figure 15: Example of custom heuristic settings maintained on detailed scheduling planning board.

As customer did not want to restrict a maximum number of orders possible per campaign, this parameter was leave it on 0 (no restrictions) on the customizing campaign profiles. The required changes were implemented on business logic on the new custom heuristic to consider minimum and maximum number of units per campaign.

If you would like to work with your own heuristics in the detailed scheduling planning board, two pre-requisites must be fulfilled to allow custom heuristics for execution:

  • The new custom heuristic must be contained on the heuristic profile assigned to the overall profile of the detailed scheduling planning board. Then, the new heuristic will appear in the list of available heuristics in the menu of interactive planning.
  • The new custom heuristic must also be maintained on the scope profile assigned to the material on the “Advanced Planning” material master view, when the “Scope Limitation” is selected as ‘PP/DS Restricted for Inhouse Only’ or ‘PP/DS Restricted’.

The heuristics algorithms other than from the above lists are not allowed.

Figure 16: Example of scope profile on “Advanced Planning” material master view.

Figure 17: Example of custom heuristic algorithm allowed on scope profile.

The standard campaign text is the description of the leading material or a fixed text in case of empty description. Customer requested the main key of the resource code (work center code) to be placed on the campaign text. This was directly coded on the new custom logic by overwriting the standard code, but it is also possible to achieve by using BADI “/SAPAPO/PCM01_TXTOPT”.

Figure 18: Example of production campaign text containing the resource key.

The planning log in PP/DS contains messages that the system issues during interactive planning or in background planning. You can use the planning log to analyze planning problems and identify the causes of the problems. On the “Settings” for the “Planning Log” on the detailed scheduling planning board you can select which types of messages you want to display on the planning log, such as termination messages or warnings or all messages.

Figure 19: Example of settings for the planning log on the detailed scheduling planning board.

Review and analysis of heuristics logs is an important activity which helps planners rectify many errors and make sure that planning results are very accurate. Customer requested a more detailed planning log containing more messages relative to specific custom logic. This was directly coded on the new custom logic by adding the specific new control messages.

Figure 20: Example of custom messages displayed in the planning log on the detailed scheduling planning board.

 

When you run PP/DS heuristic in background, you can invoke transaction /SAPAPO/RRPLOG1 to display the planning log. For interactive planning, until you save, you cannot watch the log on transaction /SAPAPO/RRPLOG1.

Figure 21: Selection screen of transaction /SAPAPO/RRPLOG1 for displaying planning logs.

 

Helpful Links

SAP Online Help for Production Campaign Planning for PP/DS

Production Campaign Planning

Functions for Production Campaign Planning

Campaign Optimization

Heuristics for Campaign Planning

Campaign Alerts

 

[Disclaimer: Written by Juan Ventura. This blog post is written by the author based on his personal experience while working on customers with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud. Before implementing any solution, you are advised to always do the analysis and adopt a solution for specific business case only if it suits customer requirements and testing is successful.]

entry.title: Event-Based Revenue Recognition: Make Period-End Close Easier with Multiple Ledger Selection
entry.content:
Period-end close can be stressful—especially when you need to run the same job multiple times for different ledgers. Until now, that’s exactly what users had to do in Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR). But good news: that’s changing.

The Problem You couldn’t run one job for several ledgers at once. You had to run each job separately, which was time-consuming and didn’t match how other accounting jobs worked.

What’s New Now you can choose more than one ledger when setting up your EBRR jobs. That means:

  • You do fewer steps at period-end close.
  • The process is faster and more aligned with other accounting tasks.

Which Jobs Are Updated These jobs now support multiple ledgers:

  • Run Revenue Recognition - Revenue Contracts (F7347)
  • Run Revenue Recognition - Sales Orders (F4276)
  • Run Revenue Recognition - Provider Contracts (F5405)
  • Run Revenue Recognition - Service Documents (F4278)
  • Run Revenue Recognition – Projects (F4277)

Important Note If your company uses a “shifted fiscal year,” you still need to check: all company code and ledger combinations must have the same fiscal year variant. If the fiscal year variants are not equal, you must schedule individual jobs for each ledger. The system will not allow you to proceed and will show an error (Message no. FINS_REV_REC246).

What It Means for You This update simplifies period-end close, helps your team save time, and reduces repetitive tasks. It's a practical step toward making your revenue recognition process more efficient and aligned with other accounting workflows—without disrupting any jobs you've already planned.

entry.title: Project scheduling in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
While Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP Cloud ERP Private and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise offers dedicated network planning and task management capabilities, SAP Cloud ERP does not provide project scheduling features as part of the core functionality. Such capabilities are provided through PRM's scheduling functionality and closely integrated with Enterprise Projects and Professional Service Projects managed in SAP Cloud ERP.  
 
Enterprise Project Scheduling: A Step-by-Step Guide

To schedule a project plan, you need to set up an enterprise project in SAP Cloud ERP as well as a collaborative project in PRM.  The second part can be done either using the “My Collaborative Projects” app in PRM or directly in the enterprise project using the related objects within the "Project Briefs" app. 

Once you have set up the project structure of your enterprise project in SAP Cloud ERP, you are ready to import the project plan into your collaborative project using the "Manage Project Plans" app. 

Through the import process, the project header, WBS elements, and milestones are imported and shown in a hierarchy that matches the source structure. Additional details, including object ID, name, processing status, and date information, are also transferred, with duration values calculated accordingly.

Note: Project plans imported using the “Manage Multiple Project Plans” app do not have the capability to be edited or scheduled. 

Since the April 2025 release, the "Manage Project Plans" app allows you (in addition to non-imported plans) to edit project plans originating from an SAP Cloud ERP system. This allows the project manager to modify the project header and its elements (= WBS elements & milestones), if their processing status in the enterprise project allows editing. Individual project elements have their own processing statuses, so while some may be restricted from changes ("Locked", "Completed" or “Closed”), others may still be editable. 

In PRM you have the possibility to set up relationships between project elements. For example, you can specify a "Finish-to-Start" dependency, to ensure that the WBS element "Discovery" is completed before the "Preparation" element can be started.  

Manual modifications can be made via the tabular view or through the drag-and-drop functionality in the gantt chart. Alternatively, you can click the “Schedule” button to utilize the bottom-up, backward scheduling algorithm. This feature takes over the project end date to the last project element and organizes all other elements based on their relationships and parent-child hierarchy. Dates from lower elements are rolled up to their parent elements, thereby calculating the project start date required to achieve the desired end date. 

Upcoming innovations  

By Q3/2025, we plan to enrich the scheduling capabilities of PRM-PM as well as deepen the integration with SAP Cloud ERP: 

  • Enhanced Interaction with SAP S/4HANA Cloud: To ensure seamless integration, the project manager will have the ability to update the dates of the respective WBS elements in SAP Cloud ERP according to the new project timeline 
  • Introducing Must Start On & Must Finish On Constraints: Define specific constraints on project elements, offering more precise control over scheduling and enhancing project flexibility. 
  • Offering Additional Scheduling Settings: Broaden scheduling strategies with options like Forward/Bottom-Up scheduling, coupled with the capability to calculate the critical path of a project plan—providing valuable insights into key tasks and timelines. 

Our Vision for Project Scheduling in SPRM - PM 

Our vision for the scheduling capabilities in SPRM-PM involves aligning business-related processes with the project timeline. This includes areas such as Resource Management and Procurement. By doing so, we aim to achieve seamless integration, efficiency, and better decision-making due to the improved visibility of dependencies and constraints to other business objects and processes.

entry.title: How to recover from a performance drop in HANA 2.0 SPS007 and SPS08
entry.content:
In my previous article Faster query execution with lesser memory now also available in SAP HANA Platform SPS07, I had explained how a  performance can degrade when an existing HEX execution exceeds a threshold limit. The plan gets recompiled to utilize one of the older engines which is called Fallback. This is to protect the system from possible regressions that may be caused by the new HEX features and a threshold limit is set. The issue is that majority would have executed in HEX with optimal performance and only a small percentage needed to be running with the older engines, but since a non-optimal plan is cached, the performance will have degraded due to the non-optimal plan in the cache.

For SPS08, we have removed fallbacks during execution if a statement is compiled and executed successfully in HEX. All subsequent executions will execute as HEX without any fallbacks. The thresholds are checked only during compilation and its first execution. If the threshold limit is reached or exceeded during compilation or the first execution, the plan will be recompiled to execute with one of the older engines. The occurrence of a fallback would be much less than SPS07 but still can occur with SPS08.

To avoid such performance drops due to fallbacks, we can utilize the auto-recompilation feature to recover from such occurrences. Once activated, it monitors any checks if there are any significant degraded performance compared to previous executions and will recompile the plan so that the plan can recover back to the optimal plan.

The feature can be enabled using the following configuration parameter to TRUE.

plan_cache_auto_recompilation_enabled 

Once enabled, the performance drop is detected based on the following logic

 

 

The average execution CPU time is computed for each N executions buckets. Basically the average from the 1st to the Nth execution compared to the next N (from N+1 th to 2N th) executions are compared. if the recent average is higher than C % of the previous execution, the plan is flagged to be recompiled at the next execution.

There can be 2 outcomes after the recompile

  1. The plan is recompiled back to HEX plans which would be the desired outcome as most executions would run faster. (left graph)
  2. The plan is recompiled again as one of the older plans which is not desired. This would be the worst case scenario as the next N executions will be similar to current N executions (right graph). For such a case, the auto recompilation cannot detect the performance drop.

 

 

 

 

The N and C are maintained as following.

N: plan_cache_auto_recompilation_rate  (default 5000 time)
C: plan_cache_auto_recompilation_compare_constant (default 1000 percent)

Them maximum recompile is limited to 5 times.

plan_cache_auto_recompilation_limit (default 5 times)

The above logic is based on following revisions.

  • SP07 Revision 79.05 or later
  • SP08 Revision 85 or later

We have disabled a logic based on number of executions which may recompile a good plan which we should avoid.

entry.title: Re: tables affected by RSADRCK2
entry.content:
RSADRCK2 report updates USR21 table from User Administration.  It also updates USADDEF table if there is any change in Default Company.

Also updates the Address tables ADRP, ADRVP.  Taking backup of these 2 tables is bit difficult as ADRVP table will have huge data.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 公共業界向けソリューション連載企画①：公共業界向けソリューション全体の紹介編
entry.content:
【第1回】公共業界向けソリューション全体の紹介編  ～ 公共業界のDXを支えるSAPソリューション ～ 

 

イントロ ダクション

本ブログは、SAP S/4HANAの公共業界向けソリューションを紹介する連載企画です。
制度対応やガバナンス強化、業務効率化といった課題に向き合う自治体・研究機関・公共法人の皆さまに向けて、予算・補助金管理業務のデジタル変革を支援する仕組みを3回にわたりご紹介してまいります。 

🔹SAP 公共業界向けソリューション連載企画（全3回） 

  1. 公共業界向けソリューション全体の紹介編 　※当ブログ 
  2. 予算・財源管理ソリューション（Funds/Budget Management）機能紹介編
  3. 補助金管理ソリューション（Grants Management）機能紹介編

SAPは、製造・流通・金融・サービス・医療・公共など25以上の業種に対応するインダストリーソリューション提供し、世界中の企業・組織の業務変革を支援してきた実績を有しています。業種ごとのビジネスモデルや制度、現場業務に精通し、業界特有のニーズに応じた最適な業務プロセスの標準化・効率化・可視化を実現しています。 
公共業界向けソリューションは、そのインダストリーソリューションの1つであり、地方自治体、中央省庁・教育機関・医療福祉法人・公益法人などを対象に、予算管理・補助金管理・財務ガバナンス・住民サービス強化といった幅広い領域で導入実績があります。 

 

はじめに：なぜ今、公共財務業務のDX（デジタルトランスフォーメーション）が求められるのか 

日本の行政組織や研究機関、公益法人など、公共領域における財務管理は、住民サービスの維持や社会インフラの継続に直結する、非常に重要な業務です。少子高齢化や財政逼迫などの背景を受け、限られた財源をいかに有効に活用できるかが問われる中で、透明性・効率性・統制性を備えた業務基盤の必要性は年々高まっています。 

公共業界における会計には、民間企業の会計とは異なる特有の特性があります。例えば、「予算に基づく資金の執行管理」が基本であり、予算制度との強い結びつきがあります。また、税金や補助金といった公的資金を扱う責任が伴うため、法令遵守やガバナンスが特に重視されます。さらに、近年は「発生主義・複式簿記」を導入する新公会計制度が進展し、従来の単式簿記・現金主義からの脱却も進んでいます。 

しかしながら、現場では依然として紙・Excel・メールを前提としたアナログ運用が根強く残っており、部門間での情報の分断や業務の非効率化、人的ミスや属人化のリスクが課題となっています。 

こうした現状を踏まえ、SAPは公共業界の財務業務に特化した2つのソリューションを提供し、現場業務の効率化と財政運営の高度化を支援しています。 
本記事では、シリーズの導入編として、SAP S/4HANAの中で公共業界向けに提供されているソリューション全体の特長と導入価値についてご紹介します。 

 

公共業界における業務課題 

公共業界のお客様が現行のシステムをご利用の上で直面する、予算・補助金管理に関する代表的な課題として、次の3点が挙げられます。 

  1. 業務効率の低下 
    • システム間の非連携やアナログ運用により、同じ情報を複数のシステムや紙・Excelなどに重複入力している 
    • 情報の集約や整合性確認に時間を要している 

  2. 情報の分散と可視性の欠如 
    • 予算・補助金の情報が部門ごとに分断管理されており、リアルタイムで最新の状況を把握することができない 
    • 結果として、意思決定の遅れや分析精度の低下が生じている 

  3. 内部統制の脆弱性 
    • 予算執行時のチェックが手作業に依存し、ミスや不正の温床になる可能性がある 
    • 監査時に必要な証跡が整理されておらず、準備作業に時間と労力を要している
       

SAPが提供する公共業界向けソリューションの全体像 

SAPは、これらの課題を包括的に解決するため、SAP S/4HANA 公共業界向けソリューションとして、2つのソリューションを提供しています。 

  • 予算・財源管理ソリューション（Funds/Budget Management） 
  • 補助金管理ソリューション（Grants Management） 

これらは1つのシステム内で、会計・調達・支払・プロジェクト管理など関連する業務とシームレスに連携しており、財務データの一元管理と業務効率化を同時に実現します。 
 
さらに、SAPではこうした業務基盤をクラウドベースで提供しており、従来のオンプレミス型システムで課題となっていた運用保守コストの削減や、自動アップデートによる制度・技術進化への迅速な対応を可能にします。
老朽化した業務システムをそのまま維持するのではなく、AIや分析機能、他サービスとの連携が容易な最新テクノロジー基盤への移行を実現することで、単なる業務改善にとどまらず、組織全体のDXを支える戦略的な基盤を構築することができます。 

 

SAP公共業界向けソリューションが提供する価値 

SAP S/4HANA 公共業界向けソリューションは、単なる業務のデジタル化に留まらず、現場とマネジメントの双方に有益な「データドリブンな財務運営」を実現します。

 本ブログでは、特長を3点でご紹介します。 

  1. 統合されたリアルタイム業務基盤
    SAP S/4HANAでは、予算執行・会計処理・調達・支払などの業務が一つの統合プラットフォームでリアルタイム連携されており、重複入力やバッチ処理の待機といった非効率を排除します。 
    たとえば、執行課の担当者が購買依頼や検収を登録すると、その情報は即座に予算に反映され、必要な会計仕訳も自動生成されます。これにより二重入力の手間が省け、業務プロセスの整合性とスピードが格段に向上します。
       
  2. 業務・分析の一体化による予算の見える化 
    業務処理の過程で発生するデータは、即時にレポートや分析画面に反映され、常に最新の情報を元に意思決定を行うことが可能になります。
     SAPの出力機能（帳票・レポート） のコンセプトは、「各担当者のニーズに応じて、豊富な切り口でレポートを自在に切り替えながら分析することができる」点にあります。
    具体的には、ドリルダウン軸やフィルタ条件（所属・科目別など）を駆使してレポートを柔軟に操作できます。
    これにより、従来の紙ベースの帳票やExcelでの加工作業から脱却し、データの正確性と意思決定の精度が向上するだけでなく、紙資源やレポーティング業務にかかるコストを削減し、職員の作業負担を軽減します。     
      
  3. 内部統制機能の強化 
    SAPシステムに入力されたデータは、権限に応じた承認プロセスを経て一貫した形で連携し、プロセス横断的に関連情報が紐づく形で保持されます。 
    例えば、支出負担行為（契約・発注などの予算引当）から支払（実際の支出処理）までの一連の取引を一元的に管理することで、「支払伝票（支払記録）から、その支払いが、どの契約・購買・予算に基づいて行われたか」を容易に追跡・確認することができます。 
    また、SAPでは、取引時点でのルール逸脱を自動的に検知・制御する仕組みが組み込まれており、ヒューマンエラーや不適切な執行を未然に防ぐことが可能です。 
    ※こちらの検知・制御機能に関しては、第2・3回で詳細をご紹介します。

     


まとめ：SAPで実現する公共業界のDX 

予算や補助金の適正な執行と情報管理は、公共組織の信頼性と持続可能性を支える基盤です。SAPは、公共業務に特化した2つのソリューションを通じて、業務の効率化・情報の見える化・内部統制の強化を同時に実現します。 

 

 

公共業界におけるDXの第一歩として、ぜひ SAP S/4HANA 公共業界向けソリューションをご検討ください。 


今後の展開：各機能の個別紹介 

本記事では、SAPの公共業界向けソリューション全体像と提供価値をご紹介しました。
次回以降は、以下のように各ソリューションの詳細にフォーカスして解説していきます。 

  • 第2回：予算・財源管理ソリューション（Funds/Budget Management）編 
    予算の編成・執行・配当・繰越といった一連の業務を支えるソリューションの全体像と主要な機能を解説します。 
  • 第3回：補助金管理ソリューション（Grants Management）編 
    補助金管理におけるライフサイクル管理・ルール制御・報告対応を支援する機能と価値をご紹介します。

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 公共業界向けソリューション連載企画②：予算・財源管理ソリューション（Funds/Budget Management）機能紹介編
entry.content:
【第2回】予算・財源管理ソリューション（Funds/Budget Management）機能紹介編

～ 業務効率化と統制強化を両立する次世代の予算管理基盤 ～


イントロ ダクション

本ブログは、SAP S/4HANAの公共業界向けソリューションを紹介する連載企画です。制度対応やガバナンス強化、業務効率化といった課題に向き合う自治体・研究機関・公共法人の皆さまに向けて、予算・補助金管理業務のデジタル変革を支援する仕組みを3回にわたりご紹介してまいります。 
第1回では、SAPの公会計ソリューション全体像と価値をご紹介しました。 
今回の第2回では「予算・財源管理ソリューション（Funds/Budget Management）*1」に焦点を当て、その特長と主要機能をご紹介します。ぜひシリーズを通してご覧ください。 

🔹SAP 公共業界向けソリューション連載企画（全3回） 

  1. 公共業界向けソリューション全体の紹介編

  2. 予算・財源管理ソリューション（Funds/Budget Management）機能紹介編　※当ブログ

  3. 補助金管理ソリューション（Grants Management）機能紹介編

*1: 予算・財源管理ソリューションは、 ご利用になるバージョンによって、呼称が異なります。 

  • S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionの場合、Funds Management  

  • S/4HANA Cloud Public Editionの場合、Budget Management  

※本ブログはSAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition を前提に作成しています。以降は、予算・財源管理ソリューション（Funds/Budget Management）を、FMと省略してご紹介します。また、登場する画面イメージ及び動画も、Private Editionを前提としています。 
※SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionと Public edition違いは下記になります。 

 

はじめに：制度改革と現場の課題 

公共業界において、予算は単なる資金計画ではなく、「政策実行・事業運営を実行するための羅針盤」としての役割を持っています。予算の適切な管理・執行が、住民サービスの質や公共インフラの維持に直結する以上、その運用の正確性・効率性は極めて重要です。 
近年、発生主義や複式簿記の導入をはじめとした制度改革が進む中、予算と会計の情報を一体的に管理し、リアルタイムで整合性を保つことが公共組織にも求められつつあります。 

しかしながら、第1弾のブログでも記載したように、実際の現場では以下のような課題が残っています。 

  • 発生主義・複式簿記の導入が進む中で、会計情報と予算情報の連携が取れていない 

  • 依然として紙・Excelベースのアナログな業務プロセスが主流 

  • 会計システムと予算システムが分断されており、二重入力や整合性チェックに多くの工数が発生 

これらの課題は、行政組織が目指す「戦略的な財務運営」や「透明性の高いガバナンス」の実現を阻む要因となっています。実際、欧州をはじめとする先進的な行政機関では、予算と実績、資金と会計、政策と事業成果を統合的に管理できる財務基盤の整備が進んでおり、公共財政の透明性と説明責任の強化に貢献しています。 

日本においても、こうした国際的な潮流に倣い、分断された業務と情報を再構築し、財務情報をリアルタイムかつ正確に把握できる基盤づくり＝予算管理のデジタル改革が急務となっています。 


Funds/Budget Managementの全体像・概要 

こうした現場の課題に対応するため、SAPでは予算・財源管理に特化したソリューション（FM）を提供しています。
FMは、単なる予算管理ツールではなく、調達・会計・出納・管理会計などの関連する業務とシームレスに連携する、統合型の予算管理プラットフォームです。　 

  • 特長①：予算執行と同時に仕訳起票 / 複式簿記への対応 
    新公会計制度では、従来の官庁会計の「現金主義・単式簿記」に代わり、「発生主義・複式簿記」を取り入れて、財務諸表を作成することが求められます。 
    SAPでは予算/官庁会計（FM）と財務/新公会計（Financial Accounting）が統合されているため、ユーザーが購買依頼や発注といった業務を実行すると、予算の差引処理と、発生主義において必要な会計仕訳が自動で計上されます。
    これにより、現場のユーザーは財務会計用の仕訳を手動で入力する必要がなくなり、人的ミスを防ぐとともに、効率的な財務管理を実現します。     

  • 特長②管理会計との連携 
    部門別、事業別、拠点別（施設別など）の単位で予算・実績情報を管理し、管理会計機能と組み合わせることで、「どの事業に、どれだけのコストがかかっているのか」を明確に可視化でき、戦略的な資源配分や政策評価にも活用できます。 

 

Funds/Budget Managementの主要機能と価値 

SAP FMは、予算のライフサイクル全体をカバーする5つの主要機能で構成されています。 
①マスタ管理、②予算編成、③予算登録、④予算執行、⑤年度末処理 

これにより、計画から執行、年度末処理までを一貫して効率的に運用することが可能です。 

① マスタ管理 
予算執行に必要な組織単位、資金源、予算科目、事業分類など、各種マスタ情報を登録・変更する機能です。マスタ情報の定義・整備によって、各組織ごとのニーズに応じた柔軟なかつ厳格な予算統制を実現します。例えば、款項目節の階層構造を持たせることで、予算を登録して管理する階層と執行する階層を分けることができます。 

 

② 予算編成 
年間の予算を、月別・四半期別・半期別といった任意の単位で計画・配当できる機能です。 
※予算編成業務は、データ分析・計画・予算編成・予測分析などを統合的に実現するクラウドプラットフォームであるSAP Analytics Cloud（SAC）の活用を推奨しています。 SACは、直感的な操作性・機械学習機能などを備え、公共業界における予算シミュレーションや計画の高度化に寄与します。 
※ SACを活用した予算編成プロセスの詳細については、こちらのブログをご参照ください。 

 

③ 予算登録 
部門・事業別に割り当てる予算の登録、補正、繰越、振替といった処理を行う機能です。
公共業界における予算管理は複雑で多様なプロセスを経て行われます。年度の初めに設定される当初予算から始まり、年度途中には補正予算や様々な予算異動（予算登録、繰越予算、項間流用、流用、所管替、予備費充用など）が実行され、年度末には予算の繰越が行われます。このように、単純に「予算」と言っても、多くの異なる種類や方法が存在します。 

FMでは、こういった予算に関わる処理に対応でき、その結果をリアルタイムでレポートに反映させることが可能です。
また、「予算タイプ」という分類で、異なる種類の予算を色分けし、管理することが可能です。 

※本動画は、デジタルアダプションツールであるWalkmeを使用したイメージです。


④ 予算執行　 
※本ブログでは歳出管理にフォーカスしていますが、歳入管理（事前調定など）にも対応しています。 
支出負担行為（契約・発注など）や支出命令（支払指図）といった公共業界特有の取引プロセスに対応し、それぞれの段階で予算残高をリアルタイムに差引・更新する機能です。 

たとえば、支出負担行為の登録時点で予算が引当され、支出命令時には実績として確定されるなど、執行状況に応じた残高管理が可能です。また、予算消化率が一定値を超えた場合には警告を出すなど、予算逸脱の防止や統制強化にも貢献します。　 
※他の関連業務（購買発注・固定資産取得・経費精算）を経由した予算執行時も、同様にリアルタイムでのチェックと制御が行われます。 

また、予算執行の状況は、リアルタイムダッシュボードを通じて可視化することができます。
部門別・費目別・時系列など、さまざまな切り口での分析が可能で、現場と管理部門の双方がタイムリーに情報を把握・共有できます。 


このように、日々の執行情報が即座に反映されることで、紙帳票や手集計に頼らず、正確かつスピーディな予算管理が実現します。 

⑤ 年度末処理 
この機能は、年度末に必要な予算の締め処理や次年度への繰越処理をサポートします。 
締め処理は、各執行予算に対して会計期間の開始と終了を月単位で定義できます。さらに、伝票の締め処理を一括で行うことも、予算科目や伝票のタイプ、所属別に実行することもできます。 

たとえば、出納整理期間中に前年度分の新規支出負担行為伝票の計上を制限したり、会計課のみが伝票整理を行えるように制御することができます。 また、日本の一般的な慣習に従い、1件ごとに繰越決議を経て、残予算の個別繰越処理を実行できます。 

 

まとめ：予算管理から行財政DXへの第一歩を

SAP Funds/Budget Managementは、公共業界特有の予算制度・運用ルールに対応した、高い柔軟性と統合性を備えたソリューションです。
紙ベースや属人的な処理から脱却し、「戦略」と「統制」を両立した予算管理体制の実現を目指す皆様にとって、極めて有効な選択肢ではないでしょうか。 

 

次回のご案内：補助金業務を支えるGrants Management 

次回のブログでは、補助金管理ソリューション（Grants Management）機能紹介編として、大学や研究機関、自治体などにおける補助金ライフサイクルのデジタル化と内部統制強化を実現するGMの特長・機能をご紹介します。 

複数の補助金制度、スポンサーごとのルール、複雑な報告要件などに悩む組織にとって、SAP GMは強力なソリューションとなるはずです。ぜひ次回もご覧ください。 

  • 第3回：補助金管理ソリューション（Grants Management）編 
    補助金管理におけるライフサイクル管理・ルール制御・報告対応を支援する機能と価値をご紹介します。

【関連ブログ】

  • 第1回：公共業界向けソリューション全体の紹介編

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 公共業界向けソリューション連載企画③：補助金管理ソリューション（Grants Management）機能紹介編
entry.content:
【第3回】補助金管理ソリューション（Grants Management）機能紹介編 

～煩雑な補助金管理を一元化し、透明性と効率を実現 ～

 

イントロダクション 

本ブログは、SAP S/4HANAに搭載された公共業界向けソリューションをご紹介する連載企画です。
制度対応やガバナンス強化、業務効率化といった課題に向き合う自治体・研究機関・公共法人の皆さまに向けて、予算・補助金管理業務のデジタル変革を支援する仕組みを3回にわたりご紹介してまいります。 

第1回では公共業界向けソリューション全体の概要を、続く第2回では予算管理を支える「Funds/Budget Management」をご紹介しました。 
最終回となる今回は、補助金管理業務に特化した「Grants Management（GM）」について、その特長と実務における効果をご紹介します。 

🔹SAP 公共業界向けソリューション連載企画（全3回） 

  1. 公共業界向けソリューション全体の紹介編

  2. 予算・財源管理ソリューション（Funds/Budget Management）機能紹介編

  3. 補助金管理ソリューション（Grants Management）機能紹介編　※当ブログ 

※本ブログに登場する画面イメージ及び動画も、第2回同様、 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition を前提に作成しています。 
※SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionと Public edition違いは下記になります。 

 

はじめに：補助金管理業務における課題 　〜複雑化と管理負荷の増大〜 

大学、研究機関、地方自治体、公益法人など、多くの公共組織にとって「補助金」は重要な資金源です。
しかし、その管理業務は年々煩雑さを増しており、制度対応やガバナンス強化が求められる一方で、現場ではその負担感が大きくなっています。

特に、次のような課題が多くの組織で共通しています。

  • 補助金の交付元（例：総務省、日本学術振興会、非営利団体など）ごとに異なる使用ルール・申請様式・報告要件への対応が求められ、業務が煩雑になりやすい 
  • 請求・精算・進捗管理が、Excelや紙によるマニュアル対応により属人化し、情報の分散や管理の非効率が常態化 
  • 「不適切な使用を防ぐためのルール遵守」と「迅速な対応・報告」を両立する業務基盤が不在 

これらの問題は、ヒューマンエラーや監査リスクを生むだけでなく、貴重な研究・行政リソースを圧迫し、補助金活用の機会損失にもつながります。
海外ではすでに、補助金管理のライフサイクル全体を一元化し、透明性と効率性を両立する仕組みの整備が進んでおり、デジタルを前提とした運用が主流になりつつあります。 

こうした流れを踏まえると、日本の公共機関においても、制度の複雑さに対応できる柔軟で信頼性の高い業務基盤の構築が急務と言えます。 


Grants Managementの全体像／概要 

このような課題を解決するのが、SAP S/4HANA公共業界向けソリューションの1つである 「Grants Management（GM）」です。
SAP GMは、補助金の草案作成から申請、執行、報告に至るまでのライフサイクル全体を一元的に管理できるソリューションです。 

さらに、Funds/Budget Management、財務会計、調達管理などの関連する業務とシームレスに連携することで、内部の会計処理プロセスを損なうことのなく、スポンサー(補助金の交付元)要件を満たすことが可能です。 
つまり、補助金と関連業務を一体化し、正確かつ効率的な業務運用及び正確でタイムリーなレポートが可能となります。 

 

 Grants Managementの主な機能と価値 

SAP GMは、補助金業務の一連の流れを構造化・効率化するために、4つの主要機能を備えています。 

  1.  スポンサー管理：業務の標準化とルール統制の基盤 
    補助金ごとの様々な情報（使用目的、対象費目、上限金額、使用期間、間接費の扱いなど）を、あらかじめマスタデータとして登録できます。
    これにより、複数の補助金を扱う場合でも、ルールを業務に自動反映し、ヒューマンエラーやルール逸脱の防止につなげることができます。 



  2. 予算・会計記帳統制 
    • 補助金予算登録： 正確な配分と柔軟な変更に対応 
      交付された補助金の金額を、プロジェクトや部門、費目に割り当てる予算登録が可能です。補正、繰越、振替といった実運用に求められる機能にも対応しています。
      また、残額や執行率もリアルタイムで確認でき、予算管理の正確性を高めることができます。 


       

       

    • 補助金執行：使用ルールに基づく自動制御で不正・ミスを未然に防止 
      GMは、単なる補助金管理ツールではなく、関連する他の業務と密に連携しているため、物品購入や経費精算など、補助金を実際に使用する段階で、あらかじめ設定された使用ルール（対象費目・執行期間）・利用可能な予算金額に基づき、リアルタイムでチェックと制御が行われます。
      このように、属人化しがちなチェック業務をシステムに任せることで、      内部統制の強化と作業負荷の軽減を両立できます。 

  3. 請求・入金管理：精算・報告業務をシステムで一元管理 
    マニュアル請求、定期請求、一括請求などスポンサーと規約に基づいた請求方法に対応可能です。 
    間接費の自動計算や、システムによる請求可能金額の自動計算によって、スポンサーへの請求業務の精度とスピードの両立が可能になります。
    また、請求後の残高確認や消込処理をSAPの財務会計機能（FI）で行うことで、未収入金管理の透明性が向上します。     


     

     

  4. レポーティング・プロジェクト完了処理：状況を“見える化”し、意思決定を支援
    補助金業務では、スポンサーごとに異なる報告書式やガイドラインへの対応が求められる場面が少なくありません。
    SA    Pでは、目的やユーザごとの気になる視点に応じた多様な切り口でのレポーティングが可能です。
    例えば、補助金の種別別・プロジェクト別・スポンサー別など、目的に応じたあらゆる切り口での表示がワンクリックで実現します。（詳しくは第1回：公共業界向けソリューション全体の紹介編をご参照ください。）     
    これにより、従来のExcel紙ベースでの管理と比較し、報告書作成にかかる時間と工数を大幅に削減でき、管理部門や現場担当者の業務負担を軽減します。

     

 

まとめ：煩雑な補助金管理業務を支える仕組み 

SAP Grants Managementは、複雑化する補助金制度と現場の実務をシステムで一元化し、制度順守・内部統制・業務効率化・透明性の向上をすべて支援できる統合型ソリューションです。 

  • 補助金の全ライフサイクルを一元的に可視化 
  • 自動チェック機能によるルール遵守とミス防止 
  • 多様な切り口のレポートで、報告や分析の精度と効率を両立 

また、第2回でご紹介したFunds/Budget Management（FM）と併せて導入することで、予算と補助金の両面をカバーする財務基盤が整い、公共財務業務全体の最適化が一層加速します。 

 

公共財務業務の未来へ向けて 

本ブログシリーズでは、全3回にわたり、公共業界における財務管理の課題とSAPソリューションをご紹介しました。 

  • 第1回：公共業界のDXを支えるSAPソリューション 
  • 第2回：予算管理業務の効率化・高度化を支援するSAP Funds/Budget Management 
  • 第3回：補助金業務を支えるGrants Management（GM）の仕組み ※当ブログ

現場と制度、業務とデータ、効率と統制を一体化し、公共組織のガバナンス強化とDX推進を後押しするSAPのソリューションを、ぜひご検討ください。 

最後までお読みいただき、ありがとうございます。
本ブログが皆様のご参考になれば幸いです。
ご要望やご質問等がございましたら、弊社営業担当またはお問い合わせ窓口までお気軽にご連絡ください
